title: 阳光下的罪恶
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\117-阳光下的罪恶-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
While discussing the existence of evil, Hercule Poirot and a group of onlookers at the Pirate's Flag Hotel observe the arrival of Elena Marshall on the beach. A former stage actress, Elena possesses a commanding and magnetic presence that attracts the attention of nearly everyone present. Patrick Redfern, a well-muscled man, is visibly drawn to her and eventually walks to her side on the sand.

The onlookers react to Elena with a mixture of fascination and hostility. Colonel Barry recalls a similar red-haired woman in Simla, India, who caused domestic chaos, while Miss Bustle labels Elena the embodiment of evil. Christiane Redfern calls her a "beast," and the vicar, Stephen Lane, becomes agitated, insisting that Elena is a menace and the incarnation of evil. Amidst this scrutiny, Poirot notes one significant exception: Elena's husband, Marshall, remains calm and does not look up from his magazine as she arrives.

Later, Poirot is joined by Rosamund Darnley, a woman of exceptional beauty and the owner of a famous London clothing company. Rosamund expresses her discomfort with her current location, noting that she had visited the area two years prior. She reveals to Poirot that she has been troubled by a painful personal vision that serves as a reminder of her past.
